title: Stretching Before the Story
summary: In the moments before the nightly 'Stars and Story' ritual, a young girl decides that her physical fitness comes first. Insisting on finishing her special exercise routine before beginning the evening's tales, she demonstrates a strong sense of priority and determination while her audience waits patiently.

highlightQuote.text: But this is my exercise, so I don't want to do Stars and Story right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daddy, but I want to do... but I want to do my special things. But this is my exercise, so I don't want to do Stars and Story right now.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you want to do some exercise right now? And then we'll do Stars and Story?

characters.name: Izzy
characters.description: A focused and determined young girl who insists on completing her workout before starting her nightly routine.

characters.name: Dad
characters.description: Kitty’s father remains a pillar of steady eccentricity, an unflappable co-pilot and accommodating listener who balances technical logistics with a patient support for his daughter’s restless need for activity. He manages mission equipment with the same meticulous intensity he once brought to scanning the midnight undergrowth for missing cats, his stoicism famously underscored by the irony of a nagging knee complaint—diagnosed as overgrown toenails—that never wavers his resolve.

places.name: The Exercise Spot
places.description: The dedicated area where Izzy performs her 'special things' and workout routines.
